provenance.source_corpus: Fertilization at planting influences seedling growth and vegetative competition on a post-mining boreal reclamation site

nodes.name: broadcast fertilization with immediately available fertilizers (IAF)

source_spans.text: broadcast fertilization with immediately available fertilizers (IAF) results in generally low rates of nutrient recovery for planted trees.

nodes.name: Directed application of controlled-release fertilizer (CRF) to the rhizosphere

source_spans.text: Directed application of controlled-release fertilizer (CRF) to the rhizosphere offers an alternative to extend nutrient longevity while reducing nutrient leaching or uptake by competing vegetation.
application of IAF also dramatically increased vegetative competition surrounding the seedlings of both species during the first growing season relative to all other treatments
White spruce year-1 height and diameter growth responded positively to both IAF and CRF relative to non-fertilized controls
No effect on seedling survival occurred.
both species had generally higher mean foliar N concentrations during the first growing season when treated with CRF vs. IAF
rapid release of nutrients immediately following application of IAF creates potential for environmental contamination through leaching.
This improves overall fertilizer use efficiency
